bill.short_title: Coronavirus Preparedness and Response Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2020

provisions.agency: Department of Health and Human Services, Food and Drug Administration
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: For an additional amount for ''Salaries and Expenses'', $61,000,000, to remain available until expended, to prevent, prepare for, and respond to coronavirus
notes: Supplemental appropriation for coronavirus response under FDA heading
Purposes include development of medical countermeasures and vaccines, advanced manufacturing for medical products, monitoring of medical product supply chains, and related administrative activities
Emergency designation under BCA section 251(b)(2)(A)(i)

cross_references.target: Section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985

provisions.program: Administrative expenses for disaster loan program under section 7(b) of the Small Business Act
provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text: For an additional amount for the ''Disaster Loans Program Account'' for administrative expenses to carry out the disaster loan program authorized by sect
notes: Supplemental appropriation for SBA disaster loans in response to coronavirus
Heading includes '(Including Transfers of Funds)'

cross_references.target: Section 7(b) of the Small Business Act
cross_references.description: Authorizing statute for the disaster loan program

cross_references.target: Section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985

provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text: Provided, That such amounts may be transferred to and merged with “Small Business Administration—Salaries and Expenses”
notes: Transfer ceiling equals the full $20,000,000 appropriation ('such amounts' refers to the entire appropriated amount)
Uses permissive 'may be transferred' language

provisions.description: For purposes of section 7(b)(2)(D) of the Small Business Act, coronavirus shall be deemed to be a disaster. Amounts available under the Disaster Loans Program Account for the cost of direct loans in any fiscal year may be used to make economic injury disaster loans under such section in response to the coronavirus.
provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text: Provided further, That for purposes of section 7(b)(2)(D) of the Small Business Act, coronavirus shall be deemed to be a disaster and amounts available
notes: Key policy provision: legally deems coronavirus a 'disaster' for SBA economic injury disaster loan (EIDL) purposes
Expands eligible uses of Disaster Loans Program Account funds to include coronavirus economic injury disaster loans

cross_references.target: Section 7(b)(2)(D) of the Small Business Act
cross_references.description: Deems coronavirus a disaster for purposes of economic injury disaster loans

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds provided under this heading in this Act for indirect administrative expenses.
provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text: Provided further, That none of the funds provided under this heading in this Act may be used for indirect administrative expenses
notes: Ensures the $20M is spent only on direct administrative costs of the disaster loan program

provisions.description: Designates the appropriated amount as an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985.
provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text: Provided further, That such amount is designated by the Congress as being for an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanc
notes: Emergency designation exempts the spending from discretionary spending caps under the BBEDCA

cross_references.target: Section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985

provisions.description: Re-designates amounts repurposed under this heading that were previously designated as emergency requirements under BBEDCA as emergency requirements pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the BBEDCA.
provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text: Provided further, That amounts repurposed under this heading that were previously designated by the Congress as an emergency requirement pursuant to the
notes: Ensures repurposed prior-year emergency funds retain their emergency designation under current BBEDCA authority

cross_references.target: Section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985

provisions.description: Funds in this title may restore pre-enactment HHS obligations for coronavirus response. Grants to States/localities/tribes shall reimburse costs incurred since January 20, 2020. Does not apply to Reserve Fund. Funds reprogrammed/transferred per the February 2, 2020 Secretary notification shall be returned to their original appropriation within 45 days of enactment.

provisions.description: Secretary of HHS may appoint candidates for critical coronavirus positions without regard to competitive examination requirements of 5 U.S.C. 3309-3319, provided public notice has been given and the Secretary has determined a public health threat exists.

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of State and USAID Administrator to jointly submit a strategy to prevent, prepare for, and respond to coronavirus abroad within 15 days of enactment, and a report on proposed uses of funds on a country and project basis within 30 days of enactment, updated every 60 days until September 30, 2022, and every 180 days thereafter until all funds are expended.
deadlines: Every 180 days thereafter until all funds expended

provisions.description: Establishes that each amount appropriated or made available by this Act is in addition to amounts otherwise appropriated for the fiscal year involved.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Each amount appropriated or made available by this Act is in addition to amounts otherwise appropriated for the fiscal year involved.
notes: Standard supplemental boilerplate clarifying these are additional funds, not replacements.

provisions.description: Prohibits any appropriation in this Act from remaining available for obligation beyond the current fiscal year unless expressly provided.

provisions.raw_text: No part of any appropriation contained in this Act shall remain available for obligation beyond the current fiscal year unless expressly so provided herei

provisions.description: Unless otherwise provided, additional amounts appropriated by this Act are available under the same authorities and conditions applicable to such appropriations accounts for fiscal year 2020.

provisions.raw_text: Unless otherwise provided for by this Act, the additional amounts appropriated by this Act to appropriations accounts shall be available under the author
notes: Ensures supplemental funds follow the same rules as existing FY2020 appropriations for each account.

provisions.description: Restricts use of all funds made available or transferred under this Act to preventing, preparing for, and responding to coronavirus, subject to specified exceptions in subsection (b) for section 301(c), reimbursements, the Emergency Reserve Fund (P.L. 115-31), and the Infectious Diseases Rapid Response Reserve Fund (P.L. 115-245).
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: (a) Subject to subsection (b), and notwithstanding any other provision of law, funds made available in this Act, or transferred pursuant to authorizatio
notes: Subsection (a) is a blanket restriction on use of funds to coronavirus purposes only.

Subsection (b) carves out exceptions for section 301(c), reimbursements, Emergency Reserve Fund (P.L. 115-31 sec. 7058(c)(1)), and Infectious Diseases Rapid Response Reserve Fund (P.L. 115-245 sec. 231).
Contains notwithstanding clause overriding other provisions of law.

cross_references.target: any other provision of law
cross_references.description: Overrides other law to restrict funds to coronavirus use

provisions.description: Defines the term 'coronavirus' as used in this Act to mean SARS-CoV-2 or another coronavirus with pandemic potential.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: In this Act, the term coronavirus means SARS–CoV–2 or another coronavirus with pandemic potential.
notes: Statutory definition that scopes the use-of-funds restriction in SEC. 504 and other provisions.

provisions.description: Conditions availability of each emergency-designated amount in this Act on the President subsequently designating all such amounts and transmitting such designations to Congress, pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985.

notes: Standard emergency designation provision requiring presidential concurrence for funds to become available.

provisions.description: Amends Section 1135(b) of the Social Security Act to grant the Secretary of HHS authority to waive or modify certain Medicare telehealth requirements (originating site and telephone restrictions) during emergency periods for services furnished by qualified providers. Adds a definition of 'qualified provider' to Section 1135(g). Authorizes implementation by program instruction or otherwise.
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 102. 
Secretarial authority to temporarily waive or modify application of certain Medicare requirements with respect to telehealth services furnished
notes: Subsection (a)(1) amends Section 1135(b) to add new paragraph (8) allowing waiver of telehealth originating-site requirements and telephone restrictions during emergencies

Subsection (a)(2) amends Section 1135(g) to add definition of 'qualified provider' — a physician/practitioner who furnished a paid item/service to the individual within the prior 3 years, or is in the same practice as one who did
Subsection (a)(3) permits implementation by program instruction

Subsection (b) clarifies definitions of 'emergency area' and 'emergency period' for purposes of new paragraph (8), tying them specifically to the January 31, 2020 COVID-19 public health emergency declaration and renewals

cross_references.target: Section 1135(b) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1320b-5(b))
cross_references.description: Adds new paragraph (8) re: telehealth waiver authority during emergencies

cross_references.target: Section 1135(g) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1320b-5(g))
cross_references.description: Adds definition of qualified provider and clarifies emergency area/period definitions

cross_references.target: Section 1834(m) of the Social Security Act
cross_references.description: Defines telehealth services referenced in new waiver authority

cross_references.target: Section 319 of the Public Health Service Act
cross_references.description: Public health emergency declaration authority referenced for emergency period definition

provisions.description: Exempts the budgetary effects of Division B from statutory PAYGO scorecards (Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010), Senate PAYGO scorecards (H. Con. Res. 71, 115th Congress), and from estimation under Section 251 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 and certain provisions of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010.
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 103. 
Budgetary effects

(a) 
Statutory PAYGO scorecards
The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO
notes: Subsection (a) exempts from statutory PAYGO scorecards under section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
Subsection (b) exempts from Senate PAYGO scorecards under section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress)
Subsection (c) exempts from budget scorekeeping under Section 251 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 and section 3(4)(C) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
Standard emergency-designated budgetary treatment provision

cross_references.target: Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines, Conference Report 105-217
cross_references.description: Overrides Rule 3 of the Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines

cross_references.target: Section 250(c)(8) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985
Section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
Section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress)
Section 251 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985

flagged_issues: Only Title II of Division A was provided for extraction; other titles/divisions of H.R. 6074 are not included in this input
Second PHSSF paragraph ($300,000,000) is conditional on Secretary certification — may not ultimately become available
CDC sub-allocations total $1,590,000,000 ($950M grants + $300M global + $300M Reserve Fund; $40M is within the $950M), leaving $610M for other CDC-directed coronavirus activities
SEC. 304 provides broad transfer authority among CDC, PHSSF, and NIH accounts with no specific dollar ceiling
All appropriations designated as emergency spending under BBEDCA section 251(b)(2)(A)(i)

SEC. 306 transfers up to $2,000,000 from Public Health and Social Services Emergency Fund to OIG; this is a ceiling on transfers from funds appropriated elsewhere in Title III, not new budget authority
Only SEC. 305 and SEC. 306 were provided from Title III; other sections in this title (e.g., appropriation accounts) were not included in the excerpt
All appropriations designated as emergency requirements under the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act
SEC. 403 increases an existing transfer authority ceiling from $10M to $100M in P.L. 116-94
$200M of the $435M Global Health Programs is mandated to transfer to the Emergency Reserve Fund
Title V contains only general provisions with no new appropriations — all spending is in other titles of the Act.
SEC. 504 restricts all funds in the Act to coronavirus purposes with limited exceptions.
SEC. 507 conditions availability of all emergency-designated funds on presidential designation.
Division B contains no appropriations — it is entirely policy riders amending Medicare telehealth law and exempting budgetary effects from PAYGO
SEC. 101 is a short title clause and was not extracted per instructions
